title: teucrium lusitanicum subsp. clementiae
intro: Teucrium lusitanicum subsp. clementiae, also known as the Portuguese germander or clementia, is a subspecies of the Teucrium lusitanicum plant, which is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Teucrium lusitanicum subsp. clementiae include reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as arthritis and neurodegenerative diseases. Teucrium lusitanicum subsp. clementiae can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
